items.title: Radical misogynist influencer Andrew Tate and brother arrested in Miami
items.standfirst: US marshals on Saturday arrested Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan in Miami as British authorities have been pushing for their extradition. The Tate brothers now face 59 charges in the UK including rape, sex trafficking, assault, tax evasion and money laundering.
items.content: US marshals on Saturday arrested Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan in Miami as British authorities have been pushing for their extradition. The Tate brothers now face 59 charges in the UK including rape, sex trafficking, assault, tax evasion and money laundering.

items.title: The Tate brothers are arrested in Miami, U.S. Marshals Service tells AP
items.standfirst: The brothers were taken into custody on a sealed warrant, the Marshals Service told the AP. British prosecutors said they were seeking the extradition of the brothers on rape and trafficking charges.

items.title: First US soldiers killed in Jordan, Trump vows to shift action
items.standfirst: Two American service members were killed and a third is missing following an Iranian attack on a US base in Jordan. The deaths are the first from hostile fire since a truce with Iran in early April and come after a week of the heaviest fighting since the conflict began in February. Saturday also marked the eighth consecutive night of US strikes on Iran, with Iranian media reporting explosions in several coastal cities. Days of escalating exchanges have targeted military installations and civilian infrastructure across the region. Morgan Aire reports.
items.content: Two American service members were killed and a third is missing following an Iranian attack on a US base in Jordan. The deaths are the first from hostile fire since a truce with Iran in early April and come after a week of the heaviest fighting since the conflict began in February. Saturday also marked the eighth consecutive night of US strikes on Iran, with Iranian media reporting explosions in several coastal cities. Days of escalating exchanges have targeted military installations and civilian infrastructure across the region. Morgan Aire reports.

items.title: US launches new Iran strikes after two soldiers killed: What’s the latest?

items.content: US strikes target Iran's IRGC after two of its soldiers were killed in Jordan, as Tehran attacks Kuwait and Bahrain.

items.title: US launches new airstrikes on Iran after confirming troop deaths
items.standfirst: Washington launched its eighth consecutive wave of strikes in Iran overnight Saturday with US Central Command saying the latest attacks had targeted Iran's Revolutionary Guard in retaliation for Tehran's killing of two US troops at an airbase in Jordan. France 24 correspondent Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.
items.content: Washington launched its eighth consecutive wave of strikes in Iran overnight Saturday with US Central Command saying the latest attacks had targeted Iran's Revolutionary Guard in retaliation for Tehran's killing of two US troops at an airbase in Jordan. France 24 correspondent Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.

items.title: Middle East live: Iran's Guards say two ships involved in 'accident' after defying Hormuz warnings
items.standfirst: Iran's Revolutionary ​Guards said on ​Sunday that two ships were involved in ​an "accident" ‌after attempting ⁠to transit the Strait ‌of Hormuz via an "unsafe route", ⁠while two other vessels abandoned ​that route, the semi-official ‌Tasnim news agency reported. The news came after Washington launched its eighth consecutive wave of strikes in Iran overnight Saturday. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: Iran's Revolutionary ​Guards said on ​Sunday that two ships were involved in ​an "accident" ‌after attempting ⁠to transit the Strait ‌of Hormuz via an "unsafe route", ⁠while two other vessels abandoned ​that route, the semi-official ‌Tasnim news agency reported. clusters.title: England beat France 6-4 to finish third
clusters.neutral_headline: England beat France 6-4 to finish third
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC Top Stories, CBS News

items.title: England beat France 6-4 to finish third
items.standfirst: The World Cup resumed on Saturday with the third-place playoff, where England defeated France 6–4. Bukayo Saka scored a hat-trick to help the Three Lions secure their best tournament finish since 1966. Despite the defeat, Kylian Mbappé scored twice for Les Bleus to overtake Lionel Messi as the all-time leading World Cup scorer with 22 career goals.
items.content: The World Cup resumed on Saturday with the third-place playoff, where England defeated France 6–4. Bukayo Saka scored a hat-trick to help the Three Lions secure their best tournament finish since 1966. Despite the defeat, Kylian Mbappé scored twice for Les Bleus to overtake Lionel Messi as the all-time leading World Cup scorer with 22 career goals.

items.title: 2026 World Cup: France fall to England after wild ten-goal thriller
items.standfirst: France endured an emotional rollercoaster in Miami on Saturday, eventually falling 6-4 to England in a breathtaking 2026 World Cup third-place playoff. Trailing 4-0 at halftime, Les Bleus mounted a sensational comeback, fueled by Kylian Mbappé's ninth and tenth goals of the tournament, but England held on to seal victory in a spectacular contest.
items.content: France endured an emotional rollercoaster in Miami on Saturday, eventually falling 6-4 to England in a breathtaking 2026 World Cup third-place playoff. Trailing 4-0 at halftime, Les Bleus mounted a sensational comeback, fueled by Kylian Mbappé's ninth and tenth goals of the tournament, but England held on to seal victory in a spectacular contest.

items.title: Watch: Highlights as England stun France 6-4 in third-place play-off
items.standfirst: Bukayo Saka scores a hat-trick as England beat France 6-4 in a thriller to record their best men's World Cup finish since 1966.
items.content: Bukayo Saka scores a hat-trick as England beat France 6-4 in a thriller to record their best men's World Cup finish since 1966.

items.title: World Cup 2026: France's Mbappé becomes all-time tournament goalscorer
items.standfirst: Kylian Mbappé made history at the World Cup by becoming the tournament's all-time leading goalscorer with 22 goals after his brace in France's third-place playoff against England. The French superstar surpassed Lionel Messi's previous record of 21 goals, cementing his legacy as one of football's greatest tournament performers.
items.content: Kylian Mbappé made history at the World Cup by becoming the tournament's all-time leading goalscorer with 22 goals after his brace in France's third-place playoff against England. The French superstar surpassed Lionel Messi's previous record of 21 goals, cementing his legacy as one of football's greatest tournament performers.

items.standfirst: Whereabouts of artist imprisoned after 2021 protests had been unknown following end of his prison term in JulyLuis Manuel Otero Alcántara, a prominent Cuban dissident artist whose whereabouts remained unknown after his prison sentence ended last week, has been given permission to travel to the US, according to his official Facebook page.Otero Alcántara, designated a prisoner of conscience by Amnesty International, was sentenced to five years in prison in 2022 for insulting national symbols, contempt and disturbing the public order. items.content: Astronomers have detected an atmosphere around LHS 1140b, a rocky exoplanet in its star’s habitable zone. The finding makes it one of the more intriguing candidates for studying potentially Earth-like conditions beyond the solar system. [bbc.com]

clusters.neutral_headline: Indian activist on hunger strike for 20 days forcibly taken to hospital
bullet_summary: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has now begun an indefinite fast in Sonam Wangchuk's place
Reported by Sky News World, BBC World

items.title: Hunger striker taken to hospital as police launch 'chilling crackdown'
items.standfirst: Police have forcibly removed hunger striker Sonam Wangchuk from a student protest site in India.
items.content: Police have forcibly removed hunger striker Sonam Wangchuk from a student protest site in India.
